stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 4 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 8 observations with mean 60.1 points (within-school SD = 3.9). school B has 10 observations with mean 48.7 points (within-school SD = 4.6). school C has 10 observations with mean 52.4 points (within-school SD = 8.3). school D has 15 observations with mean 54.7 points (within-school SD = 3.1). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.7 and 19.2 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school D: 54.7 points (shrunk from raw 54.7). Range [54.7, 54.800000000000004] as between-school SD varies from 7.7 to 19.2. Pooled mean: 53.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.8 points (weighted average of 4 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=12.8: B = 0.996.
Interval [54.7, 54.800000000000004] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (54.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
